moat.narrative: MA economics are gated by CMS Star Ratings (4+ stars unlocks ~5% quality bonus) — Humana's historical 4.5-star scale was a structural revenue advantage that competitors couldn't replicate. CenterWell's 350 senior primary-care clinics let Humana capture clinical risk in-house at 15-20% better MLR than fee-for-service settings, creating a self-reinforcing flywheel.

key_customers: CMS (counter-party for all MA/PDP premiums — effectively a single buyer)
US Department of Defense (TRICARE East region)
~5.6M individual MA members across the US
key_risks: Star Ratings cliff — 2025 plans rated 4+ stars dropped sharply, meaningfully reducing 2026 quality bonus payments; ongoing CMS lawsuit unresolved
CMS v28 risk-adjustment model phase-in (final year 2026) cuts coding revenue; mgmt targets 'at least 3% MA pretax margin' by 2027 but flags uncertainty
Individual MA membership guided to decline 'up to 500,000' in 2025 as Humana exits unprofitable counties — open question whether margin recovery materialises

SNX.company_overview: TD SYNNEX is the world's largest IT distributor (post-2021 Synnex/Tech Data merger), reselling endpoint solutions (PCs, mobile, peripherals, print) and advanced solutions (data center, hybrid cloud, cybersecurity, networking, hyperscale) from ~2,500 vendors to ~150,000 reseller, MSP and integrator customers in 100+ countries. It earns gross-margin spread plus higher-margin services like cloud marketplace orchestration (StreamOne) and Hyve hyperscale ODM manufacturing. FY25 revenue $62.5bn (+7%).

revenue_mix.segment: Advanced Solutions (data center, security, cloud, networking — incl. Hyve hyperscale)

end_markets: Managed Service Providers (MSPs) — cloud marketplace driven

moat.type: Vendor relationships at duopoly scale + Hyve manufacturing
moat.narrative: TD SYNNEX and Ingram Micro form a global IT-distribution duopoly that vendors must sell through to reach long-tail resellers — replicating the line card and 100-country logistics is a multi-billion dollar barrier. Hyve hyperscale ODM (custom server/storage for the cloud titans) is a unique vertical asset competitors don't have, and StreamOne cloud marketplace captures recurring SaaS billing economics.

key_customers: Hyperscalers (named Hyve customers — historically Meta, Microsoft) for ODM kit
Microsoft, Cisco, Dell, HPE, Lenovo, AWS as largest line-card suppliers
key_risks: Vendor margin compression — Microsoft/Cisco shifting to direct-CSP and consumption-billing models reduces distributor take rate
Hyve customer concentration — hyperscaler in-housing of ODM design (Meta's MTIA, AWS Graviton) could erode the segment's strategic moat
Cyclical PC/endpoint refresh exposure — Endpoint Solutions (~50% of mix) tied to commercial PC replacement cycles that pulled forward into 2024-25

TCAP.L.company_overview: TP ICAP is the world's largest inter-dealer broker (IDB), matching wholesale OTC trades for banks, hedge funds and asset managers across rates, credit, FX, money markets, equities, and energy & commodities. It earns brokerage commissions per transaction (voice + hybrid + electronic), plus a fast-growing data/analytics business (Parameta) selling proprietary OTC pricing data, and a buy-side block-trading venue (Liquidnet) for equities/credit. FY25 revenue £2.4bn (+6% cc).
end_markets: Global investment banks (sell-side desks — JPM, GS, MS, Citi, etc.)
Quant funds & risk vendors buying Parameta data
Central banks for OTC reference prices

moat.type: Broker relationships + venue network effects
moat.narrative: IDB economics depend on broker rolodexes — TP ICAP's ~3,000 brokers carry decades of relationships with sell-side desks that cannot be replicated; broker poaching by BGC is the central competitive battleground (and risk). Parameta data is structurally unique: it's the only place to buy proprietary OTC pricing in markets like esoteric rates and emerging-market FX, with no electronic-venue equivalent.

key_customers: Global Tier-1 investment banks (top 20 sell-side desks)
Buy-side asset managers using Liquidnet for block execution
Commodity majors and trading houses (E&C client base)
Risk vendors and quant funds licensing Parameta data
key_risks: E&C segment revenue fell 2-7% in FY25 due to broker departures to rivals — talent retention costs eating margin
Continued electronification of credit and rates flow (Tradeweb, MarketAxess gaining share) compresses voice-broking economics over time
Parameta spin-off / IPO repeatedly delayed — execution overhang on valuation that depends on data-business re-rating

sources.label: Finance Magnates: TP ICAP FY2025 results
